message:
fix(linalg): replace magic epsilon constants with scale-relative thresholds and add matrix function iteration limits

Major numerical robustness improvement across the linalg module:

Eigenvalue/eigenvector back-substitution now uses scale-relative thresholds
(eps * matrix_inf_norm) instead of fixed 1e-10/1e-15/1e-30 magic constants.
This prevents both false zero-detection in large-norm matrices and premature
truncation in small-norm matrices. Adds matrix_inf_norm() helper used by
extract_schur_eigenvalues and eig_nxn_full back-substitution.

Replaces all magic iteration limits with named constants: SVD_QR_ITERATION_COEFF
(100), EIGEN_QR_ITERATION_COEFF (60), SQRTM_MAX_ITERATIONS (50),
MATRIX_FUNC_TAYLOR_TERMS (30), LOGM_MAX_SCALING_ITERATIONS (20). Each has a
doc comment explaining its role and safe tuning range.

Matrix exponential convergence check changed from absolute (term < 1e-16) to
relative (term_norm < eps * result_norm), fixing premature termination for
large-norm inputs and unnecessary iteration for small-norm inputs.

Conformance module imports RNG budget constants (MAX_RNG_JUMP_OPERATIONS,
MAX_RNG_STATE_SCHEMA_FIELDS) from fnp-random instead of duplicating them,
ensuring the conformance tests and the RNG module agree on limits.

IO module gains doc comments on all public safety constants (MAX_HEADER_BYTES,
MAX_ARCHIVE_MEMBERS, etc.) explaining the threat model each defends against.

FEATURE_PARITY.md updated with matrix_transpose, cumulative_sum,
cumulative_prod, trapezoid additions to NumPy 2.0 API row.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
